name: You.com Search API
what_it_does: AI-powered web search API with RAG-ready results, news, code search, and grounded snippets for agent knowledge retrieval.
use_cases: Ground LLM responses with live web search results to reduce hallucination
Retrieve current news and events for real-time agent awareness
Search developer documentation and code repositories for coding agents
Build research agents that need structured web snippets with source citations
Augment RAG pipelines with live web context beyond static knowledge bases
not_for: High-volume scraping or web crawling at scale
Structured data extraction requiring full HTML parsing
Applications requiring free-tier web search without API key
best_when: Best for agents needing grounded, citation-ready web search results with AI-synthesized snippets.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need full web page content rather than summarized snippets.

auth.notes: API key passed as X-API-Key header. Keys generated from you.com developer portal.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-query pricing; higher tiers unlock news, code, and AI snippet endpoints
pricing.notes: Pricing depends on endpoint type (web, news, code, AI snippets). Check developer portal for current rates.

performance.rate_limits: Tier-dependent; starter plans have low QPM limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are basic HTTP status codes with minimal detail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search queries are read-only and idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: Different endpoints (web, news, code, AI snippets) require separate API calls and may have different pricing tiers
AI snippet results are summarized and may omit exact source quotes — always check raw snippets for citations
Rate limits are per-plan and not clearly documented per endpoint — test before production use
Search results are English-centric; non-English queries may return fewer or lower-quality results
The you_com_rag_results field in AI endpoints is the most useful for RAG — raw search results need more processing

security_score_components.security_notes: API key only — no OAuth or fine-grained scopes. Key rotation not automated.
